entries.definition:
d–s–s to shove in, to infiltrate, to hide in, to adulterate, to
bury; a spy, a plot, intrigue; sand adder. Of this root, يَدُسُّ yaduss
occurs 11 times in the Qur’an. (Qur’anic word دَسَّاهَا dassāhā is not
considered here, as it is viewed by a number of philologists as an
elongated form of دَسَّى dassā).

يَدُسُّ yaduss [imperf. of v. دَسَّ dassa, trans.] to bury, to shove in
(16:59) أَيُمْسِكُهُ عَلَى هُونٍ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرَابِ should he keep it [the
newborn girl] in humiliation (or, and suffer humiliation himself)
or bury her in the dust?

دَسَّاهَا dassāhā (see د/س/ي d–s–y).
